flashcards.back: A frame allocation strategy where a process receives a static number of frames that remain unchanged during execution.
A state where excessive page faults occur, causing CPU utilization to drop dramatically and system performance to degrade.

flashcards.front: What is the Working Set Model?
flashcards.back: A dynamic allocation approach that assigns frames based on the pages a process has recently used (working set).
Improves memory utilization by adapting frame allocation to a process’s changing needs.
Accessing a page not present in memory, requiring it to be loaded from disk into a free frame.
